2605.00267.title: Jailbroken Frontier Models Retain Their Capabilities
2605.00267.paper_summary: As language model safeguards become more robust, attackers are pushed toward developing increasingly complex jailbreaks. Prior work has found that this complexity imposes a "jailbreak tax" that degrades the target model's task performance. We show that this tax scales inversely with model capability and that the most advanced jailbreaks effectively yield no reduction in model capabilities. Evaluating 28 jailbreaks on five benchmarks across Claude models ranging in capability from Haiku 4.5 to Opus 4.6, we find Haiku 4.5 loses an average of 33.1% on benchmark performance when jailbroken, while Opus 4.6 at max thinking effort loses only 7.7%. We also observe that across all models, reasoning-heavy tasks display considerably more degradation than knowledge-recall tasks. Finally, Boundary Point Jailbreaking, currently the strongest jailbreak against deployed classifiers, achieves near-perfect classifier evasion with near-zero degradation across safeguarded models. We recommend that safety cases for frontier models should not rely on a meaningful capability degradation from jailbreaks.
2605.00267.paper_summary_zh: 隨著語言模型的安全措施變得更加健全，攻擊者被迫開發越來越複雜的越獄手段。先前的研究發現，這種複雜性會施加一種「越獄稅」，從而降低目標模型的任務表現。我們顯示這種稅收與模型能力呈反比，最先進的越獄手段實際上不會有效降低模型能力。我們對五個基準測試中的28個越獄手段進行評估，這些基準測試涵蓋了從 Haiku 4.5 到 Opus 4.6 的 Claude 模型，我們發現 Haiku 4.5 在越獄後基準表現平均損失 33.1%，而 Opus 4.6 在最大思考努力下僅損失 7.7%。我們還觀察到，在所有模型中，重推理任務的性能下降顯著高於知識回憶任務。最後，邊界點越獄（Boundary Point Jailbreaking），目前對已部署分類器的最強越獄手段，實現了近乎完美的分類器逃避，並在受到保護的模型中幾乎沒有性能下降。我們建議，對前沿模型的安全案例不應依賴於越獄所帶來的顯著能力下降。
2605.00267.author: Daniel Zhu et.al.
2605.00267.authors: Daniel Zhu, Zihan Wang, Jenny Bao, Jerry Wei
